title: Auditing Material 3 Before Expressive Ships — I Need Your Input
author: Saikiran Chepuri
summary:
I'm doing a structured UX audit of Google Material 3's interaction
design — specifically micro interactions (state layers, ripple, FAB
morph, switch/checkbox animations) and macro interactions (Container
Transform, Shared Axis, Navigation Bar/Rail, Predictive Back).

M3 Expressive lands with Android 16 around September 2025 with bolder
motion, richer shapes, and faster animations. Before it does, I want a
clear picture of what's actually broken in current M3 — from devs and
designers who work with it daily, not just the spec docs.

Already found 9 structured findings from GitHub issues alone — the
Container Transform return path is broken in 3 separate ways.

If you've touched M3 in Compose, Views, Figma, or as a Pixel user,
4 minutes here would help a lot:
full_text:
I'm doing a structured UX audit of Google Material 3's interaction

 design — specifically micro interactions (state layers, ripple, FAB

 morph, switch/checkbox animations) and macro interactions (Container

 Transform, Shared Axis, Navigation Bar/Rail, Predictive Back).

M3 Expressive lands with Android 16 around September 2025 with bolder

 motion, richer shapes, and faster animations. Before it does, I want a

 clear picture of what's actually broken in current M3 — from devs and

 designers who work with it daily, not just the spec docs.

Already found 9 structured findings from GitHub issues alone — the

 Container Transform return path is broken in 3 separate ways.

If you've touched M3 in Compose, Views, Figma, or as a Pixel user,

 4 minutes here would help a lot: https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLScb2o19Xt0MrnF0Uj4JkmScgYo3mFdXgodtX7jCON9DGhPStA/viewform?usp=header

I'll share the full audit findings publicly when done — both the

 framework and the data.

Tags: #android #ux #materialdesign #jetpackcompose #design
